## Understanding Reading Skills and Objectives
ଓଡ଼ିଆ: ଏହି ଅଧ୍ୟାୟରେ, ଆମେ ଇଂରାଜୀ ପଢ଼ିବା ଦକ୍ଷତା ଏବଂ ଏହାର ଲକ୍ଷ୍ୟଗୁଡ଼ିକୁ ବୁଝିବା। ଏହା ଆମକୁ କିପରି ଭଲ ପାଠକ ହେବାରେ ସାହାଯ୍ୟ କରିବ, ତାହା ମଧ୍ୟ ଜାଣିବା।

Reading is a fundamental skill (ମୌଳିକ ଦକ୍ଷତା) in learning English. For Class 8 students, developing strong reading skills means you can understand different types of texts and gather information effectively. This lesson outlines the key objectives (ଲକ୍ଷ୍ୟ) and how you can achieve them.

### 1. Key Reading Indicators (Learning Objectives)
ଓଡ଼ିଆ: ଏହି ବିଭାଗରେ ଆମେ ପଢ଼ିବା ଦକ୍ଷତାର ମୁଖ୍ୟ ଲକ୍ଷ୍ୟଗୁଡ଼ିକୁ ଦେଖିବା, ଯାହା ଆମେ ହାସଲ କରିବା ଉଚିତ୍।

As a learner, you are expected to achieve the following reading competencies:

*   **Comprehension of Prescribed Texts:** You should be able to read and understand poems and paragraphs from your textbooks with $80\%$-$90\%$ comprehension (ବୁଝିବା ଶକ୍ତି). This means grasping the main idea and important details.
*   **Understanding Public Instructions:** You can read and comprehend instructions found in public places (ସର୍ବସାଧାରଣ ସ୍ଥାନ) like signs or notices with $90\%$-$95\%$ comprehension. For example, instructions at a bus stop.
*   **Guessing Unfamiliar Words:** You can guess the meanings of new or unknown words from the surrounding context (ପ୍ରସଙ୍ଗ) with $60\%$-$80\%$ comprehension. This skill helps you read without stopping frequently.
*   **Predicting Text Content:** You can read a prose text and predict what might happen next with $60\%$ correctness. This shows active engagement with the story.
*   **Reading Authentic Materials:** You can read and understand various real-world English materials (ପ୍ରକୃତ ସାମଗ୍ରୀ) like newspapers, magazines, or simple stories not from your textbook, with $70\%$-$80\%$ comprehension.
*   **Locating Specific Information:** You can read documents like railway time tables (ରେଳ ସମୟ ସାରଣୀ), forms, or brochures (ପୁସ୍ତିକା) and find specific information with $70\%$-$80\%$ comprehension. This is a practical skill for daily life.
*   **Fast Reading Skills (Non-detailed Study):**
    *   Read and comprehend larger sections of text (ବଡ଼ ଅଂଶ) at once.
    *   Quickly guess the meaning of difficult words using context.
    *   Read approximately $50$ words per minute with $70\%$ comprehension.
    *   **Skimming (ଶୀଘ୍ର ପଢ଼ିବା):** Quickly read through a text to get the overall idea (ମୁଖ୍ୟ ଧାରଣା) or main point.
    *   Identify the storyline and important

words that guide the narrative.
    *   Form mental pictures (ମାନସିକ ଚିତ୍ର) of descriptions (ବର୍ଣ୍ଣନା) and enjoy the reading process.

### 2. Suggested Pedagogical Processes (How to Improve)
ଓଡ଼ିଆ: ଏହି ବିଭାଗରେ ଆମେ ପଢ଼ିବା ଦକ୍ଷତା ବୃଦ୍ଧି କରିବା ପାଇଁ ଶିକ୍ଷକମାନେ କିପରି ପଦ୍ଧତି ଅବଲମ୍ବନ କରିବେ, ତାହା ଜାଣିବା।

To help you develop these skills, your teacher will use various methods:

*   **While-reading and Post-reading Activities:** Engaging in tasks both during (while-reading) and after (post-reading) you read a text. These activities help deepen your understanding.
*   **VMDT Activities:** Using Visual (ଦୃଶ୍ୟ), Motor (ଶାରୀରିକ), Dynamic (ଗତିଶୀଳ), and Tactile (ସ୍ପର୍ଶ) activities to make learning more interactive and memorable.
*   **Reading Authentic Materials:** Practicing with real-world English texts, not just textbook lessons.
*   **Reading Instructions:** Focusing on understanding instructions given in your textbooks and other materials.
*   **Exposure Classes:** Participating in classes designed to expose you to different types of English reading materials and situations.
*   **Answering Prediction Questions:** Before or during reading, answering questions about what you think will happen next. This makes you an active reader.
*   **Increasing Chunk Length:** Gradually reading longer and longer sections of text to improve your endurance and comprehension of larger pieces.
*   **Developing Skimming Skills:** Practicing how to quickly read for the main idea, often through activities that involve identifying the storyline.

### Example: Guessing Meaning from Context
ଓଡ଼ିଆ: ଏଠାରେ ଆମେ ଏକ ଉଦାହରଣ ଦେଖିବା ଯେ କିପରି ଏକ ଅଜଣା ଶବ୍ଦର ଅର୍ଥ ପ୍ରସଙ୍ଗରୁ ଅନୁମାନ କରିହେବ।

Consider the sentence:
(ବହୁତ ପୂର୍ବରୁ). These words suggest something very old. So, you can guess that

means very old or from a long time ago.

### Flow of Reading Comprehension
ଓଡ଼ିଆ: ଏଠାରେ ଆମେ ପଢ଼ିବା ଏବଂ ବୁଝିବାର ପ୍ରକ୍ରିୟାକୁ ଏକ ସରଳ ଚିତ୍ର ମାଧ୍ୟମରେ ଦେଖିବା।

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Start Reading Text] --> B{Encounter Unfamiliar Word?}
    B -- Yes --> C[Use Context Clues (ପ୍ରସଙ୍ଗ ସୂଚନା)]
    C --> D{Guess Meaning}
    B -- No --> E[Continue Reading]
    D --> E
    E --> F{Comprehend Main Idea & Details}
    F --> G[Locate Information]
    G --> H[Predict Next Event]
    H --> I[Finish Reading]
```

By practicing these skills and engaging in the suggested activities, you will become a more confident and effective English reader.

## Let's Make Reading Fun and Easy!
ଓଡ଼ିଆ: ଆସନ୍ତୁ ପଢ଼ିବାକୁ ମଜାଦାର ଏବଂ ସହଜ କରିବା। ଏହି ପାଠରେ ଆମେ କିପରି ଭଲ ପାଠକ ହେବା ତାହା ଶିଖିବା।

Hello, Class 8 student! Reading in English is like having a superpower. It helps you learn new things, enjoy stories, and understand the world around you. This lesson will show you what good readers can do and how you can become one too!

### What Good Readers Can Do (Your Reading Goals)
ଓଡ଼ିଆ: ଜଣେ ଭଲ ପାଠକ କଣ କରିପାରିବେ, ତାହା ଏଠାରେ ଦିଆଯାଇଛି। ଏଗୁଡ଼ିକ ଆପଣଙ୍କର ପଢ଼ିବା ଲକ୍ଷ୍ୟ।

*   **Understand Stories and Poems:** You will be able to read stories and poems from your book and understand most of it (about $80\%$-$90\%$). This means knowing what is happening and who the characters are.
*   **Follow Instructions:** Imagine you are at a railway station (ରେଳ ଷ୍ଟେସନ). You should be able to read signs or time tables (ସମୟ ସାରଣୀ) and understand them very well (about $70\%$-$95\%$). This helps you find your way.
*   **Guess New Words:** Sometimes you see a word you don't know. A good reader can guess its meaning (ଅର୍ଥ ଅନୁମାନ କରିବା) by looking at the other words in the sentence (ପ୍ରସଙ୍ଗ). You'll get good at this ($60\%$-$80\%$ of the time)!
*   **Predict What Happens Next:** When you read a story, you can often guess what will happen next. This makes reading more exciting and shows you are thinking about the story.
*   **Read Quickly for Main Ideas (Skimming):** Sometimes you don't need to read every single word. You can quickly look over a text (ଶୀଘ୍ର ପଢ଼ିବା) to get the main idea (ମୁଖ୍ୟ ଧାରଣା). This is called skimming.
*   **Find Information:** You can quickly find specific details in a text, like a phone number in an advertisement or a date in a news article.
*   **Read Faster:** You'll aim to read about $50$ words in one minute and understand $70\%$ of it. This is a great skill for reading longer books!

### How We Will Practice (Easy Steps to Improve)
ଓଡ଼ିଆ: ଆପଣଙ୍କ ପଢ଼ିବା ଦକ୍ଷତାକୁ ଉନ୍ନତ କରିବା ପାଇଁ ଆମେ କିପରି ଅଭ୍ୟାସ କରିବା, ତାହା ଏଠାରେ ଦିଆଯାଇଛି।

We will do fun activities to help you become a better reader:

*   **Before and After Reading:** We will do activities before we start reading to get ready, and after we finish to check our understanding.
*   **Real-World Reading:** We will read things like simple newspaper articles or brochures (ପୁସ୍ତିକା) – not just your textbook. This is called reading

(ପ୍ରକୃତ ସାମଗ୍ରୀ).
*   **Guessing Games:** We will practice guessing the meanings of new words from the sentences they are in.
*   **Prediction Questions:** Your teacher will ask you what you think will happen next in a story. This helps you think deeply about what you are reading.

### Simple Example: Guessing a Word
ଓଡ଼ିଆ: ଏକ ସରଳ ଉଦାହରଣ ଦେଖିବା ଯେ କିପରି ଏକ ଶବ୍ଦର ଅର୍ଥ ଅନୁମାନ କରିହେବ।

If you read:
(ବିରାଟ). But you know elephants are big and
suggests it's a large animal. So, you can guess

means very, very big.

Keep practicing, and you'll soon be a super reader!

## Guiding Your Child's English Reading Journey
ଓଡ଼ିଆ: ଆପଣଙ୍କ ପିଲାର ଇଂରାଜୀ ପଢ଼ିବା ଯାତ୍ରାରେ କିପରି ସାହାଯ୍ୟ କରିବେ, ତାହା ଏଠାରେ ଦିଆଯାଇଛି।

Dear Parent,

Your child is in Class 8, and this is a crucial time for developing strong English reading skills. As a guide, your role is to help them learn independently, not just memorize. This lesson outlines what your child needs to achieve in reading and how you can support them at home.

### What Your Child Needs to Learn (Reading Skills)
ଓଡ଼ିଆ: ଆପଣଙ୍କ ପିଲାକୁ କେଉଁ ପଢ଼ିବା ଦକ୍ଷତା ଶିଖିବାକୁ ପଡିବ, ତାହା ଏଠାରେ ଦିଆଯାଇଛି।

Your child should be able to:

*   **Understand Textbooks:** Read and comprehend (ବୁଝିବା) poems and paragraphs from their English textbook with good understanding (around $80\%$-$90\%$).
*   **Follow Instructions:** Read and understand instructions in public places (ସର୍ବସାଧାରଣ ସ୍ଥାନ) or on forms (ଫର୍ମ) and railway time tables (ରେଳ ସମୟ ସାରଣୀ) with high accuracy ($70\%$-$95\%$).
*   **Guess New Words:** When they encounter an unfamiliar word (ଅଜଣା ଶବ୍ଦ), they should try to guess its meaning (ଅର୍ଥ ଅନୁମାନ କରିବା) from the surrounding sentences (context). This is a very important skill.
*   **Predict and Locate Information:** They should be able to guess what might happen next in a story and find specific details (ସୂଚନା ଖୋଜିବା) quickly in any text.
*   **Read Different Materials:** Encourage them to read beyond textbooks – simple stories, articles, or brochures (ପୁସ୍ତିକା) in English.

### How You Can Help (Supportive Activities)
ଓଡ଼ିଆ: ଆପଣ କିପରି ଆପଣଙ୍କ ପିଲାକୁ ସାହାଯ୍ୟ କରିପାରିବେ, ତାହା ଏଠାରେ ଦିଆଯାଇଛି।

*   **Read Together:** Choose a simple English story or newspaper article. Read a paragraph aloud, then ask your child to read the next. Pause and ask them about what they just read.
*   **Ask Questions:** Instead of giving answers, ask open-ended questions. For example,

*   **Encourage Real-World Reading:** Point out English signs, instructions on product labels, or simple forms. Ask your child to read them and explain what they mean.
*   **Focus on Understanding, Not Just Speed:** While speed is important (they aim for $50$ words/minute with $70\%$ comprehension), understanding comes first. If they read too fast and don't understand, slow them down.
*   **Review Activities:** After reading a story, ask them to summarize it in their own words or identify the main characters and events.

Remember, your encouragement and patience are key. Let them try to figure things out first before offering help. This builds their confidence and skills.

questions: What was the main idea of this paragraph?
Can you find the specific information about [X] in this text?
What do you think the word '[new word]' means here, looking at the other words?
If this story continues, what do you predict will happen next?
Can you tell me in your own words what these instructions mean?
What did you enjoy most about reading this piece?
tips: Let your child struggle for 30 seconds before offering a hint.
Encourage them to re-read a sentence or paragraph if they don't understand it.
Praise their effort and improvement, not just correct answers.

## Exam Preparation: Reading Skills for Class 8
ଓଡ଼ିଆ: ଅଷ୍ଟମ ଶ୍ରେଣୀ ଛାତ୍ରଛାତ୍ରୀଙ୍କ ପାଇଁ ପଢ଼ିବା ଦକ୍ଷତା ପରୀକ୍ଷା ପ୍ରସ୍ତୁତି। ଏହା ପରୀକ୍ଷାରେ ଭଲ କରିବା ପାଇଁ ଗୁରୁତ୍ୱପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣ ସୂଚନା ଦେବ।

This section provides a focused guide for mastering reading skills for your BSE Odisha Class 8 English examination. Success depends on understanding question patterns and avoiding common pitfalls.

### What gets asked
ଓଡ଼ିଆ: ପରୀକ୍ଷାରେ କେଉଁ ପ୍ରକାରର ପ୍ରଶ୍ନ ପଚରାଯାଏ, ତାହା ଏଠାରେ ଦିଆଯାଇଛି।

*   **Comprehension Questions:** Direct questions based on prescribed poems/paragraphs, requiring $80\%$-$90\%$ understanding of main ideas and details.
*   **Information Location Tasks:** Questions asking to find specific data from authentic materials like railway time tables, forms, or brochures, targeting $70\%$-$80\%$ accuracy.
*   **Contextual Vocabulary:** Identifying the meaning of unfamiliar words from a given passage, assessing your ability to infer meaning from context ($60\%$-$80\%$ comprehension).
*   **Prediction-based Questions:** Asking what might happen next in a prose text or story, testing your ability to anticipate plot developments ($60\%$ correctness).
*   **Skimming for Main Idea:** Questions requiring you to quickly read a text (especially non-detailed study passages) and state its overall theme or main point.

### Common mistakes
ଓଡ଼ିଆ: ସାଧାରଣତଃ ଛାତ୍ରଛାତ୍ରୀମାନେ କେଉଁ ଭୁଲ୍ କରନ୍ତି, ତାହା ଏଠାରେ ଦିଆଯାଇଛି।

*   Not reading the entire passage or poem thoroughly before attempting questions.
*   Failing to use surrounding words (context) to guess the meaning of new vocabulary.
*   Misinterpreting instructions in public notices or forms due to hurried reading.
*   Providing answers that are too brief or not directly supported by the text.
*   Spending too much time on a single difficult question, impacting overall time management.

### Scoring hints
ଓଡ଼ିଆ: ପରୀକ୍ଷାରେ ପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣ ମାର୍କ ପାଇବା ପାଇଁ କିପରି ଉତ୍ତର ଲେଖିବେ, ତାହା ଏଠାରେ ଦିଆଯାଇଛି।

*   **First Read-Through:** Always read the entire text once to get a general understanding, then re-read carefully for details.
*   **Underline Key Information:** As you read, underline or highlight important names, dates, places, and main points relevant to potential questions.
*   **Context Clues for Vocabulary:** When encountering an unknown word, look at the words before and after it to infer its meaning; don't leave it blank.
*   **Direct Answers:** For comprehension questions, provide answers directly from the text, using your own words where appropriate but staying true to the source.
*   **Practice Speed and Accuracy:** Aim to read around $50$ words per minute with $70\%$ comprehension for non-detailed texts; regular practice improves both.

### Practice prompts
ଓଡ଼ିଆ: ଏଠାରେ କିଛି ଅଭ୍ୟାସ ପ୍ରଶ୍ନ ଦିଆଯାଇଛି, ଯାହାକୁ ଆପଣ ବର୍ତ୍ତମାନ ଚେଷ୍ଟା କରିପାରିବେ।

1.  Read a short paragraph from your textbook and identify its main idea in one sentence.
2.  Look at a railway timetable and find the departure time for a specific train.
3.  Read a sentence with an unfamiliar word and guess its meaning using context.
